sound_type: musical laughing call -- high-pitched yodeling 'kla-ha' or 'kow-yow' at 500-3000 Hz, liquid laughing cadence, most musically complex goose call in PNW, flock sounds like distant laughter, E=4 flock-amplified musical quality
ecosystem: Field, winter visitor. Agricultural fields, flooded pastures, and wetlands in PNW lowlands. Breeds on Arctic tundra. Circumpolar distribution.
season: Winter visitor (October-March). Breeds on Arctic tundra in summer. Migration through PNW in fall and spring.

coordinates.description: Reference for Skagit Valley agricultural fields. Greater White-fronted Geese winter in mixed flocks with Canada Geese, Snow Geese, and swans.

taxonomy_note.description: FIFTEENTH consecutive Anatidae (118-132). Unprecedented territory. First Anser (gray goose) genus in the engine. Genus shift from Branta (black geese, 129-131) to Anser (gray geese, 132). E=4 reflects the musical, laughing, yodeling quality of the call. Fourth goose in the goose era (129-134). 'Specklebelly' nickname from dark belly markings. Circumpolar distribution.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Anatidae: fifteen consecutive at degrees 118-132. Nearly doubled the Picidae record (9).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Anser from Latin 'goose.' albifrons from Latin albi ('white') + frons ('forehead/front') -- 'white-fronted goose,' referring to the white band at the bill base.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Anser -- first entry. The gray goose genus. Genus shift from Branta (129-131) to Anser (132). Distinct visual and acoustic character from the black geese.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Anseriformes. Fifteenth consecutive Anatidae. The streak enters unprecedented territory.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Anatidae at fifteenth consecutive. Four genera (various duck genera, Cygnus, Branta, Anser). Three subfamilies. Nearly doubled the Picidae record.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 500-3,000 Hz. Highest-pitched goose in the engine. Higher than Canada Goose (200-2,500), Cackling Goose (400-2,000), and Brant (200-1,200).
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 700-1,800 Hz. Higher energy center than other geese. The bright, carrying quality comes from this higher pitch center.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Laughing call: 500-3,000 Hz. Alarm: sharper, more rapid. Flight call: same laughing quality.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual calls 0.5-1.5 seconds. Two to three syllables with rising pitch. Given in series.
song_metrics.note_structure: MUSICAL LAUGHING CALL: High-pitched yodeling 'kla-ha' or 'kow-yow' or 'kla-hah-luk.' Pitch rises on second syllable. Liquid, musical quality. Distinctly resembles human laughter.
song_metrics.note_types: Laughing call (primary, the defining sound), alarm call (sharper, more urgent), flight call (laughing quality maintained), contact call (softer version between pair members).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Series of laughing calls with moderate gaps. In flocks, the overlapping laughter creates a musical chattering chorus unlike any other goose flock sound.
song_metrics.timbre: High. Liquid. Musical. Yodeling. The pitch modulation (rising and falling) creates the laughing quality. More complex structure than Branta honks/cackles/murmurs.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Individual call is musical and carrying but not overwhelming. Flock chorus is distinctive and pleasant. Comparable to Cackling Goose and Brant at E=4 but musically different.
song_metrics.tempo: Moderate. Laughing calls at 2-4 second intervals. Faster when alarmed. The rhythm has a cheerful quality.
song_metrics.context: Flock communication, flight coordination, alarm, pair bonding. The laughing call is used in all social contexts. Often the first detection cue in mixed goose flocks.
song_metrics.regional_variation: North American and Eurasian populations have similar vocalizations. Minimal geographic variation. The call is largely innate rather than learned.
song_metrics.key_property: MUSICAL LAUGHING CALL -- E=4 MOST MUSICALLY COMPLEX GOOSE CALL. High-pitched yodeling 'kla-ha' at 500-3,000 Hz. Liquid, laughing cadence. Flock sounds like distant laughter. FIFTEENTH CONSECUTIVE ANATIDAE (118-132) -- UNPRECEDENTED. First Anser genus. Genus shift Branta to Anser. 'Specklebelly' -- dark belly markings. Pink-orange bill with white band. Orange legs. Medium size. Circumpolar distribution. Arctic breeder. Agricultural field specialist in winter.

plumage.overall: Gray-brown overall. No black head or neck (unlike Branta). White band at bill base ('white-fronted'). Dark belly bars and blotches ('specklebelly'). Pink-orange bill. Orange legs and feet.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: White band at bill base. Dark belly markings. Pink-orange bill. Orange legs. Gray-brown head (no black). Unique appearance among PNW geese.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Adults: belly markings develop with age (juveniles lack them). Juvenile: no white front, no belly markings, duller overall.
plumage.flight_pattern: V-formation in flocks. Laughing flight calls. Gray-brown appearance in flight (no black head/neck of Branta). Orange bill and legs visible at close range.

physical_description.overall: Body length 64-81 cm (25-32 inches). Weight 1.9-3.3 kg (4.2-7.3 lbs). Wingspan 130-165 cm. Medium-sized goose -- larger than Cackling and Brant, smaller than Canada Goose.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: White band at bill base. Speckled belly. Pink-orange bill. Orange legs. No black head. In mixed flocks, the gray head and colored bill are immediately distinctive.
physical_description.field_id: Listen for the laughing call first -- it stands out in mixed goose flocks. Look for: gray-brown head (no black), pink-orange bill with white band, orange legs, speckled belly. The combination is diagnostic.

nodes.title: Musical Laughing Call -- E=4 Most Complex Goose Call
nodes.description: High-pitched yodeling 'kla-ha' at 500-3,000 Hz. Liquid, laughing cadence. Most musically complex goose call in PNW. Flock sounds like distant laughter. Voice as fastest detector in mixed flocks.

nodes.title: First Anser Genus -- Gray Geese Enter the Engine
nodes.description: Genus shift from Branta (black geese, 129-131) to Anser (gray geese, 132). Gray-brown head, pink-orange bill, orange legs. Visual shift parallels acoustic shift: laughing vs. honking/cackling/murmuring. A fundamentally different goose.

nodes.title: The Specklebelly -- Identification and Field Marks
nodes.description: Dark belly bars and blotches = 'specklebelly' nickname. White band at bill base = 'white-fronted.' Pink-orange bill. Orange legs. Medium size (1.9-3.3 kg). Circumpolar distribution. Arctic tundra breeder. Agricultural field specialist in winter.

nodes.title: Goose Quartet Position 4 (129-132)
nodes.description: Canada (129, deep honk, E=5, Branta), Cackling (130, higher cackle, E=4, Branta), Brant (131, low murmur, E=4, Branta), Greater White-fronted (132, musical laugh, E=4, Anser). Four geese, four sounds, two genera. Goose era continues toward Snow Goose (133) and Ross's Goose (134).

nodes.title: Fifteenth Consecutive Anatidae (118-132) -- Unprecedented
nodes.description: Nearly doubled the Picidae record (9). Post-climax E=4 plateau for three entries (130-132). Four genera. Three subfamilies. Ducks, swans, Branta geese, Anser geese. The streak enters uncharted territory.

cross_references.relationship: GOOSE ERA! Brant (131, E=4, marine murmur, Branta) to White-fronted (132, E=4, field laughter, Anser). Both E=4, different habitats, different genera. The genus shift from Branta to Anser.
GOOSE QUARTET! Canada (129, E=5, deep honk), Cackling (130, E=4, higher cackle), Brant (131, E=4, low murmur), White-fronted (132, E=4, musical laugh). Four sounds, two genera, one family streak.
FIFTEEN CONSECUTIVE! Harlequin (118, E=5, stream) to White-fronted (132, E=4, field). Fifteen consecutive Anatidae. Nearly doubled the Picidae record.
E=4 PLATEAU! Trumpeter (128, E=6 climax). After descent: E=4, E=4, E=4 for three entries (130-132). The post-climax plateau stabilizes.
NEARLY DOUBLED! Picidae held 9 consecutive (107-115). Anatidae now at 15 (118-132). Six more than the previous record. The streak is running away.
